revision.comment: Importing Wikidata [[Wikipedia:Short description|short description]]: "Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine"
revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

[[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

3]][[en:Takehara Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] {{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

3]][[en:Takehara Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] {{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. {{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[category:kk]] [[category:kk]][[category:Takehara Kofun]][[category:kk]] [[category:qq]][[category:Takehara Kofun]][[category:qq]] [[category:qq]][[category:Takehara Kofun]][[category:qq]]

{{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. {{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. {{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Takehara kofun.png | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}} [[file:Takehara kofun 04.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Takeharu Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the [[Four Symbols]] of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the [[Vermilion Bird]] and [[Black Tortoise]] less clear. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, [[horse harness]]es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from [[Goguryeo]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of [[Nōgata Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
