<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takehara Kofun]][[en:Takehara Kofun]][[ja:竹原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Takehara Kofun]][[en:Takehara Kofun]][[ja:竹原古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takehara Kofun]][[en:Takehara Kofun]][[ja:竹原古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11599019]] Wikidata name: Takehara Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese 6th-century tomb at Suwa Shrine}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Takehara Kofun | native_name =竹原古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Takehara kofun 04.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takehara Kofun murals | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takehara Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.73325|N|130.610139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Miyawaka|WD=Q990431}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|43|59.7|N|130|36|36.5|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the {{ill|Four Symbols|en|Four Symbols|WD=Q1073260|lt=Four Symbols}} of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the {{ill|Vermilion Bird|en|Vermilion Bird|WD=Q1059392|lt=Vermilion Bird}} and [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Tortoise]] less clear. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness}}es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of {{ill|Nōgata Station|en|Nōgata Station|WD=Q7071610|lt=Nōgata Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhō Main Line|en|Chikuhō Main Line|WD=Q872540|lt=Chikuhō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of {{ill|Nōgata Station|en|Nōgata Station|WD=Q7071610|lt=Nōgata Station|1=Nōgata Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhō Main Line|en|Chikuhō Main Line|WD=Q872540|lt=Chikuhō Main Line|1=Chikuhō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Takehara Kofun'''|竹原古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Takehara neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Miyawaka, Fukuoka|Miyawaka]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191952|title=竹原古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takehara Kofun is a {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} circular tumulus with a diameter of approximately 17.5 meters and height of six meters, located within the grounds of a Suwa Shrine. The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the {{ill|Four Symbols|en|Four Symbols|WD=Q1073260|lt=Four Symbols|1=Four Symbols}} of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the {{ill|Vermilion Bird|en|Vermilion Bird|WD=Q1059392|lt=Vermilion Bird|1=Vermilion Bird}} and [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Tortoise]] less clear. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness|1=Horse harness}}es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of {{ill|Nōgata Station|en|Nōgata Station|WD=Q7071610|lt=Nōgata Station|1=Nōgata Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhō Main Line|en|Chikuhō Main Line|WD=Q872540|lt=Chikuhō Main Line|1=Chikuhō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site is located on a hill between the Kuromaru River and the Yamaguchi River, which are tributaries of the Inunaki River. It was discovered in March 1956. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal entry stone chamber with a total length of 7.7 meters, orientated to the south. It consists of an anterior chamber and a posterior chamber. The side walls were built with broken stones, and the posterior chamber is approximately 2.7 meters long, 2.2 meters wide, and 3 meters high, and the anterior chamber is approximately 1.2 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 2.2 meters high. It is a [[decorated kofun]] and its noted for its excellent mural, which is painted on the huge monolithic stone forming the back wall of the posterior chamber. This mural is approximately 1.5 by 2 meters wide and has clear colors of black and red. the design consists of geometric designs, such as triangles, waves and small boats, a representation of a funeral ritual with noblemen in attendance, and a horse race. The mural also contains the {{ill|Four Symbols|en|Four Symbols|WD=Q1073260|lt=Four Symbols|1=Four Symbols}} of [[Taoism]], of which the [[Azure Dragon ]] is in good preservation, with the {{ill|Vermilion Bird|en|Vermilion Bird|WD=Q1059392|lt=Vermilion Bird|1=Vermilion Bird}} and [[:en:Black Tortoise|Black Tortoise]] less clear. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, glass beads, metal rings, {{ill|Horse harness|en|Horse harness|WD=Q3127570|lt=Horse harness|1=Horse harness}}es (bridles, apricot leaves, crosspiece fittings, etc.), and weapons (sword scabbards, iron arrowheads). The tumulus is believed to date from the latter half of the 6th century, and to show a strong influence from {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is open to the public on Mondays. The tumulus is approximately 13 kilometers west of {{ill|Nōgata Station|en|Nōgata Station|WD=Q7071610|lt=Nōgata Station|1=Nōgata Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhō Main Line|en|Chikuhō Main Line|WD=Q872540|lt=Chikuhō Main Line|1=Chikuhō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
